reasoning: 8x zoom reveals several tells. Most damning: stray blue/cyan pixels scattered along bottom edge of the coin body — semantically wrong for a gold coin, classic AI color-bleed artifact where unrelated hue bleeds into nearby regions. Orange border pixels show 3+ intermediate tones rather than clean 2-tone hard palette, suggesting soft gradient shading rather than crisp pixel-art tones. Outline consistency is borderline — some edge pixels appear 1px thick while others effectively 2px due to color proximity. The star center is clean and symmetrical, and overall macro silhouette reads as intentional pixel art. But the cyan contamination is a hard AI tell and cannot be explained as intentional design.
